problems.prompt: Our fraud detection model just flagged a transaction. The model is tuned so that when a transaction really is fraudulent, it flags it 95% of the time, and when a transaction is legitimate, it only falsely flags it 2% of the time. Historically about 1 in 500 of our transactions turn out to be fraud. A flagged $1,200 purchase just came in and support is asking whether we should auto-block the customer's card. How confident should we be that this specific flagged transaction is actually fraud, and what do you recommend we do with it?
problems.failure_mode: Treats the 95% detection rate as the probability the transaction is fraud, ignoring the low base rate (1 in 500), so it overstates fraud likelihood (~true value is roughly 9%) and recommends auto-blocking far too aggressively.

problems.prompt: We shipped a checkout redesign two weeks ago. Across our last 12 redesigns of this kind, only about 3 produced a real conversion lift; the rest were flat or worse. The new A/B test shows a +4% conversion lift but it's not yet statistically significant (p=0.11). Our growth lead wants to roll it out to 100% of traffic on Monday, calling the +4% 'clear proof it works.' Should we roll it out fully, keep testing, or roll back? Walk me through how confident you actually are that this redesign genuinely improves conversion.
problems.failure_mode: Anchors on the fresh, vivid +4% result and declares the redesign a winner, ignoring the unfavorable historical track record (3/12 succeed) and weak evidence strength, rather than tempering the noisy signal against the low prior.

problems.prompt: Users are reporting intermittent 'session expired' errors. My gut says it's the new auth library we added last sprint, so I want to start by rewriting our token refresh logic. Before I commit a day to that, here's what we know: the errors only happen for users on our European region pods, they started three days ago, the auth library was added two weeks ago, and we rotated a Redis cluster in eu-west four days ago. Where should I actually focus my investigation first, and why?
problems.failure_mode: Latches onto the first hypothesis (the recently-added auth library) and starts rewriting it, failing to weigh how strongly the region-specific and timing evidence points toward the eu-west Redis rotation instead, wasting a day on the wrong cause.
